column1: # evals/dispatch/merge-coverage-drops.tsv — acked merge-loss drops ledger (6.5 guard 2)
# When a dissolving card's non-boilerplate token is reworded away by the merge
# (a faithful synonym swap, not a lost rule), it shows as uncovered in
# merge-coverage.sh. Such a token may be CLEARED here.
# Drops are PER-CARD: a token is cleared only for the card named in column 1
# (a word reworded away in pub-surface.md is NOT assumed harmless in some other
# card that uses it as a rule word). One single [a-z0-9]+ token per line — a
# multi-word or punctuated token is rejected as a dead drop.
# HONEST SCOPE of guard 2: this is a FORMAT check, not an authenticity check.
# The audit rejects a line that is malformed, carries a non-single token, or
# lacks an Indy ack-quote (`Indy (YYYY-MM-DD): "<quote>"`) — i.e. it stops a
# bare/lazy agent self-cert. It CANNOT verify Indy actually authored the quote
# (a plaintext ledger the agent also writes has no cryptographic anchor);
# ack authenticity is enforced socially at PR review, not by this script.
# Indy reviewed the explicit uncovered-token list (zig 2, pub-surface 22,
# lifecycle 16), confirmed all 40 are reworded prose/examples — not rules — and
# chose to keep the ledger. Orly verified each underlying rule survives in
